data.title: Nissan to cut production of top-selling Rogue SUV in response to Trump tariffs - report
TomTom GAAP EPS of €0.02, revenue of €140.4M
data.description:
• TomTom N.V. press release (OTCPK:TMOAF): Q1 GAAP EPS of €0.02.
  • Revenue of €140.4M (+0.8% Y/Y).
  • Location Technology revenue increased by 2% to €121 million (Q1 '24: €119 million).
  • Automotive operational revenue increased by 5% to €83 million (Q1 '24: €79 million).
  • Free cash flow was an outflow of €3 million (Q1 '24: outflow of €9 million).
  • Net cash of €257 million (Q4 '24: €264 million).

data.title: Publicis Groupe reports Q1 results; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Publicis Groupe S.A. press release (OTCQX:PUBGY): Q1 Revenue of €3.54B (+9.6% Y/Y), including organic growth of +4.9%.
  • The Groupe’s liquidity remains very solid at €4.2 billion.
  • Reaffirming full year 2025 guidance: Organic growth of +4 to +5% o Slight improvement in margin vs. industry-high level of 18% in 2024 o €1.9 to €2.0 billion free cash flow generation.

data.title: Bank of America to pay $540.3M in a long-running legal fight with FDIC
Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson GAAP EPS of SEK1.24, revenue of SEK55.03B; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson press release (NASDAQ:ERIC): Q1 GAAP EPS of SEK 1.24.
  • Revenue of SEK 55.03B (+3.2% Y/Y). Organic sales were stable Y/Y.
  • Adjusted gross margin was 48.5% (42.7% last year).
  • Adjusted EBITA was SEK 6.9B, with a 12.6% margin.
  • Free cash flow before M&A was SEK 2.7B.
  • "Looking ahead, we remain confident of our strong position in Mobile Networks and expect Enterprise to stabilize during 2025. In the evolving global trade landscape and macro volatility, we continue to focus on controlling what we can control and delivering to our customers."
  • The proposed dividend for 2024 of SEK 2.85 per share was approved by the AGM on March 25, 2025. The dividend will be paid in two installments. The first dividend payment of SEK 1.43 per share was made with the record date of March 27, 2025, with a payment date of April 1, 2025. The second dividend payment of SEK 1.42 per share will be made with the record date September 29, 2025, with an expected payment date of October 2, 2025.
  • Outlook: Segments:There remains increased uncertainty on the outlook for Q2 2025, both in terms of potential for further tariff changes and in the broader macroeconomic environment.
  • Networks Sales growth in Q2 2025 is expected to be broadly similar to 3-year average seasonality. Based on our current assessment of announced tariffs, and including a negative impact from tariffs estimated at approximately 1 percentage point, adjusted gross margin in Q2 is expected to be in the range of 48% to 50%.
  • Cloud Software and Services Sales growth in Q2 2025 is expected to be broadly similar to 3-year average seasonality.
  • Amortization of intangible assets is expected to be around SEK -0.5 b. per quarter, of which approximately SEK -0.4 b. related to segment Enterprise.
  • Restructuring charges for 2025 are expected to remain at elevated levels.

data.title: Cue Biopharma stock falls after pricing capital raise of $20M via securities offering
Mullen Group to acquire Cole Group in logistics expansion
data.description:
Mullen Group (OTCPK:MLLGF) is set to purchase the shares of Cole Group Inc., Cole International Inc., Abco International Freight Inc., and all associated companies (collectively "Cole Group") to strengthen its logistics services.

Cole Group, a leading Canadian and U.S. logistics firm specializing in customs brokerage, freight forwarding, and trade consulting.

"The development will boost Mullen Group’s U.S. and International Logistics segment, aligning with its strategic growth in the logistics sector. Cole Group will continue operating under its current leadership," said Murray K. Mullen, Chair and Senior Executive Officer.

Mullen Group intends to fund this transaction through its existing cash and credit facilities.

The parties have filed the necessary regulatory submissions and expect the transaction to close in the second quarter.

data.title: NAYA Biosciences to separate fertility and oncology businesses into distinct operations, changes name to INVO Fertility
data.description:
INVO Fertility, formerly known as NAYA Biosciences (NASDAQ:NAYA) has announced a strategic separation of its fertility and oncology businesses, with the public company being renamed to INVO Fertility, Inc., effective April 14.

The company's Nasdaq ticker will change from NAYA to IVF. However, it will provide additional information on the expected date for the new symbol to go effective.

As part of a strategic realignment, the company plans to divest a majority of ownership in its wholly owned subsidiary, NAYA Therapeutics Inc. (NAYA TX). Simultaneously, its oncology division will become a private entity named NAYA Therapeutics (developing bifunctional antibodies), with INVO holding a minority interest for potential gains that could be generated from the clinical development of NAYA TX's bifunctional antibodies. 

The restructuring of INVO Fertility involves the public company concentrating on fertility clinic operations and expansion, distributing the FDA-cleared INVOcell device, and managing its current revenue-generating centers in Wisconsin, Alabama, and Georgia.

The move is expected to enable both businesses to maximize shareholder value.

The final separation is subject to completing definitive transaction documents and key closing conditions, including receipt of necessary approvals.

data.title: Tuesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Empire State Manufacturing Index 
 
The New York Fed conducts monthly survey of manufacturers in New York State. Participants from across the state represent various industries. On the first of each month, the same pool of roughly 200 manufacturing executives is sent a questionnaire to report the change in an assortment of indicators from the previous month. 
 
The index is expected at minus 10.0 in April after falling to minus 20.0 in March from 5.7 in February and minus 12.6 in January.
 
8:30 AM Import and Export Prices 
 
Import price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods that are bought in the United States but produced abroad and export price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods sold abroad but produced domestically.
 
Import prices are expected flat on the month and export prices up 0.1 percent on the month in March after rising 0.4 percent and 0.1 percent in February. 
 
11:35 AM Fed's Barkin Speech 
 
Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak on"Driving Through the Economic Fog" before a luncheon at the University of North Carolina, Pembroke.
 
7:10 Fed's Cook Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook will give 2025 Distinguished Alumna Award acceptance remarks before the Cal Alumni Club of Washington, D.C. Annual Reception.
